title_en: Salah: Conditions, Pillars, and Obligations of Prayer
content_en:
Salah (prayer) is the second pillar of Islam and the first act of worship for which a person will be held accountable on the Day of Judgment. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'The first matter that the servant will be held accountable for on the Day of Resurrection is the prayer. If it is correct, the rest of his deeds will be correct; if it is corrupt, all of the rest of his deeds will be corrupt' (at-Tabarani).

In Zad al-Mustaqni', al-Hajjawi outlines the conditions (shurut) for the validity of prayer. These are: Islam, sound intellect, reaching the age of discernment (tamyiz), removing ritual impurity (hadath), removing physical impurity (najasah) from the body, clothing, and place of prayer, covering the 'awrah, facing the qiblah, entering the prayer time, and intention (niyyah).

The pillars (arkan) of prayer number fourteen in the Hanbali school: the standing posture (qiyam) when able, the opening takbir, recitation of Surah al-Fatihah, bowing (ruku') with tranquility (tuma'ninah), rising from ruku', prostration (sujud) on seven body parts with tranquility, sitting between the two prostrations with tranquility, the final tashahhud, sitting for the final tashahhud, invoking blessings on the Prophet in the final sitting, the two salams, and maintaining the proper sequence (tartib) throughout.

The obligations (wajibat) of prayer, distinct from pillars in that their omission may be compensated by the prostration of forgetfulness (sujud as-sahw), number eight: all the takbirat other than the opening one, saying 'Sami'a Allahu liman hamidah' (for the imam and one praying alone), saying 'Rabbana wa laka al-hamd,' saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal-'Azim' once in ruku', saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal-A'la' once in sujud, saying 'Rabbi ighfir li' between the two prostrations, the first tashahhud, and sitting for the first tashahhud.

The Hanbali school distinguishes carefully between pillars, obligations, and sunnahs (recommended acts). Omitting a pillar intentionally or forgetfully without completing it makes the prayer invalid. Omitting an obligation intentionally makes the prayer invalid; if done forgetfully, it is compensated by sujud as-sahw. Omitting a sunnah does not invalidate the prayer but diminishes its reward.

The times of the five daily prayers are established by Quranic verses and prophetic hadith. Fajr runs from true dawn until sunrise. Dhuhr begins after the sun passes its zenith and ends when the shadow of every object equals its length plus its noon shadow. 'Asr begins at that point and extends until sunset (with the preferred time ending when shadows double in length). Maghrib begins at sunset and extends until the red twilight disappears. 'Isha begins at that point and extends until midnight, though delay beyond midnight is makruh.

The Friday prayer (Jumu'ah) replaces Dhuhr for free, sane, adult, resident males. It requires a minimum of forty qualified attendees in the Hanbali school, two khutbahs before the prayer, and congregation (jama'ah). The two khutbahs must include praise of Allah, blessing upon the Prophet, exhortation to taqwa, and a Quranic verse.
content_id:
Shalat adalah pilar Islam kedua dan amal ibadah pertama yang akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban seseorang pada Hari Kiamat. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Amal pertama yang akan dihisab seorang hamba pada Hari Kiamat adalah shalatnya. Jika shalatnya baik, maka baiklah seluruh amalnya; jika shalatnya rusak, maka rusaklah seluruh amalnya' (at-Thabarani).

Dalam Zad al-Mustaqni', al-Hajjawi menguraikan syarat-syarat (syuruth) keabsahan shalat. Syarat-syarat tersebut adalah: Islam, akal sehat, mencapai usia tamyiz, menghilangkan hadats, menghilangkan najasah dari badan, pakaian, dan tempat shalat, menutup aurat, menghadap kiblat, masuknya waktu shalat, dan niat.

Rukun-rukun shalat berjumlah empat belas dalam madzhab Hanbali: berdiri (ketika mampu), takbiratul ihram, membaca Surah Al-Fatihah, ruku' dengan thuma'ninah, bangkit dari ruku', sujud di atas tujuh anggota badan dengan thuma'ninah, duduk di antara dua sujud dengan thuma'ninah, tasyahhud akhir, duduk untuk tasyahhud akhir, bershalawat atas Nabi ﷺ pada duduk akhir, dua salam, dan menjaga urutan yang benar (tartib) selama shalat.

Kewajiban-kewajiban (wajibat) shalat, yang berbeda dari rukun karena kelalaiannya dapat dikompensasi dengan sujud sahwi, berjumlah delapan: semua takbir selain takbiratul ihram, mengucapkan 'Sami'a Allahu liman hamidah' (bagi imam dan orang yang shalat sendiri), mengucapkan 'Rabbana wa laka al-hamd,' mengucapkan 'Subhana Rabbiyal-'Azhim' sekali saat ruku', mengucapkan 'Subhana Rabbiyal-A'la' sekali saat sujud, mengucapkan 'Rabbi ighfir li' di antara dua sujud, tasyahhud pertama, dan duduk untuk tasyahhud pertama.

Madzhab Hanbali membedakan dengan cermat antara rukun, wajib, dan sunnah (amalan yang dianjurkan). Meninggalkan rukun secara sengaja atau karena lupa tanpa menyelesaikannya membuat shalat tidak sah. Meninggalkan wajib secara sengaja membuat shalat tidak sah; jika dilakukan karena lupa, dikompensasi dengan sujud sahwi. Meninggalkan sunnah tidak membatalkan shalat tetapi mengurangi pahalanya.

Waktu-waktu shalat lima waktu ditetapkan oleh ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an dan hadis-hadis Nabi ﷺ. Subuh berlangsung dari fajar shadiq hingga terbit matahari. Zhuhur dimulai setelah matahari melewati zenit dan berakhir ketika bayangan setiap benda sama dengan panjangnya ditambah bayangan tengah hari. Ashar dimulai pada saat itu dan berlanjut hingga terbenamnya matahari (dengan waktu utama berakhir ketika bayangan dua kali lipat panjangnya). Maghrib dimulai saat terbenam matahari dan berlanjut hingga merahnya awan di cakrawala hilang. Isya dimulai pada saat itu dan berlanjut hingga tengah malam, meskipun mengakhirkan setelah tengah malam hukumnya makruh.

Shalat Jumat menggantikan shalat Zhuhur bagi laki-laki yang merdeka, berakal, dewasa, dan mukim. Dalam madzhab Hanbali, shalat Jumat memerlukan minimal empat puluh jamaah yang memenuhi syarat, dua khutbah sebelum shalat, dan berjamaah. Kedua khutbah harus mencakup pujian kepada Allah, shalawat atas Nabi ﷺ, nasihat untuk bertaqwa, dan sebuah ayat Al-Qur'an.
content_ar: الصلاة (الصلاة) is the ثانياً pillar of الإسلام and the أولاً act of worship for which a person will be held accountable on the يوم القيامة. النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) said: 'The أولاً matter that the servant will be held accountable for on the Day of البعث is the الصلاة. إذا it is correct, the rest of his deeds will be correct; إذا it is corrupt, all of the rest of his deeds will be corrupt' (at-Tabarani). In Zad al-Mustaqni', al-Hajjawi outlines the conditions (shurut) for the validity of الصلاة. These are: الإسلام, sound العقل, reaching the age of discernment (tamyiz), removing ritual impurity (hadath), removing physical impurity (النجاسة) from the الجسد, clothing, and place of الصلاة, covering the 'العورة, facing the القبلة, entering the الصلاة time, and intention (niyyah). The pillars (arkan) of الصلاة number fourteen in the الحنبلي school: the standing posture (qiyam) عندما able, the opening التكبير, التلاوة of السورة al-Fatihah, bowing (الركوع') with السكينة (tuma'ninah), rising from الركوع', prostration (السجود) on seven الجسد parts with السكينة, sitting between the two prostrations with السكينة, the final التشهد, sitting for the final التشهد, invoking البركات on النبي ﷺ in the final sitting, the two salams, and maintaining the proper sequence (tartib) throughout. The obligations (wajibat) of الصلاة, distinct from pillars in that their omission may be compensated by the prostration of forgetfulness (السجود as-sahw), number eight: all the takbirat other than the opening one, saying 'Sami'a Allahu liman hamidah' (for the الإمام and one praying alone), saying 'Rabbana wa laka al-hamd,' saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal-'Azim' حين in الركوع', saying 'Subhana Rabbiyal-A'la' حين in السجود, saying 'Rabbi ighfir li' between the two prostrations, the أولاً التشهد, and sitting for the أولاً التشهد. The الحنبلي school distinguishes carefully between pillars, obligations, and sunnahs (recommended acts). Omitting a pillar intentionally or forgetfully without completing it makes the الصلاة invalid. Omitting an الالتزام intentionally makes the الصلاة invalid; إذا done forgetfully, it is compensated by السجود as-sahw. Omitting a السنة does not invalidate the الصلاة but diminishes its الأجر. The times of the five daily الصلوات are established by القرآني الآيات and prophetic الحديث. Fajr runs from true dawn حتى sunrise. Dhuhr begins بعد the sun passes its zenith and ends عندما the shadow of every object equals its length plus its noon shadow. 'Asr begins at that point and extends حتى sunset (with the preferred time ending عندما shadows double in length). Maghrib begins at sunset and extends حتى the red twilight disappears. 'Isha begins at that point and extends حتى midnight, though delay beyond midnight is المكروه. The Friday الصلاة (الجمعة) replaces Dhuhr for free, sane, adult, resident males. It requires a minimum of forty qualified attendees in the الحنبلي school, two khutbahs
